name: Instatus Status Page API
what_it_does: Status page and incident management platform with REST API — enabling software teams to create public and private status pages, manage incidents, schedule maintenance windows, and send notifications to subscribers via email, SMS, Slack, and webhooks, as a lightweight, affordable alternative to Atlassian Statuspage.
use_cases: Creating and updating incident reports via Instatus API for automated incident management
Posting component status updates via Instatus API for real-time system health communication
Scheduling maintenance windows via Instatus API for proactive customer communication
Integrating monitoring alerts with Instatus API for automatic incident creation from alerts
Managing subscriber notifications via Instatus API for status page automation
not_for: Internal-only operations dashboards without customer-facing status needs
Complex enterprise incident management with ITSM workflows (use PagerDuty or ServiceNow)
Monitoring and alerting (Instatus is status page only, not a monitoring tool)
best_when: A startup or SMB needs a customer-facing status page with incident management — where Instatus provides a Statuspage alternative at a fraction of Atlassian's pricing with a developer-friendly REST API.
avoid_when: You need full ITSM incident workflows, internal monitoring/alerting, or are already on Atlassian ecosystem (use Statuspage).

auth.notes: API key via Authorization: Bearer header. API key from Instatus account settings. No OAuth2. No fine-grained scopes. Webhooks for status change and incident events. REST API for all status page management. No rate limit details publicly documented.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free plan: 1 status page, up to 100 subscribers
pricing.notes: Egyptian company (Cairo). Founded 2020 by Mohamed Elbadry. Bootstrapped and profitable. Atlassian Statuspage alternative. 10,000+ status pages. Very developer-friendly API. Simple pricing (much less than Statuspage). Custom domain support on all paid plans. White-label (no Instatus branding on paid). Subscriber notifications: email, SMS, Slack, Teams, Discord, webhook. Public and private pages. Multi-language support. Uptime monitoring integration (basic). Growing quickly in developer community. Product Hunt featured.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST API with Bearer token. Webhooks. No OAuth2. Simple pricing. Free tier for testing. Egyptian company. Good developer documentation. Status page alternative to Atlassian Statuspage.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Status page reads idempotent. Incident creation not idempotent — check for existing open incident before creating. Incident update idempotent by incident ID. Component status updates idempotent by component ID.
known_agent_gotchas: Page ID required for all API calls — each status page has unique page ID (find in dashboard URL)
Incident status lifecycle: investigating → identified → monitoring → resolved — must follow this flow
Webhook events include status changes and incident updates — verify webhook signature
Free tier limit (100 subscribers) — upgrade needed for production use with many users
Egyptian company — US data hosting; check data residency if EU compliance required
API rate limits not documented — implement exponential backoff for safety

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced. Bearer token auth. No OAuth2 scope granularity. US data residency. Status page data is typically public information. Webhook signature validation available. No formal compliance certifications documented. Bootstrapped startup — limited security audit history.
